title: AI That Fixes Itself: Inside CodeMender, Google DeepMind’s New Agent That Patches Vulnerabilities…
author: Kamal Acharya
text:
Member-only story
AI That Fixes Itself: Inside CodeMender, Google DeepMind’s New Agent That Patches Vulnerabilities Automatically
Meet CodeMender, an autonomous AI system that doesn’t just find software vulnerabilities, it fixes them, validates the patches, and rewrites code to make entire classes of bugs impossible.
Modern software runs everything from critical infrastructure to smartphones, but it’s also riddled with vulnerabilities. Despite decades of security automation, zero-day exploits continue to emerge faster than humans can patch them.
Traditional techniques like fuzzing and static analysis can find bugs, but they struggle with scale and complexity. As AI models become more capable of reasoning about code, a new frontier has opened: autonomous software repair.
Enter CodeMender, a research project that signals what might be the biggest leap yet toward self-healing software.
What Is CodeMender?
Developed over the past six months by researchers leveraging the Gemini Deep Think models, CodeMender is an AI-powered agent designed to automatically detect, analyze, and fix software vulnerabilities.
